name: mc_knn_standardization_zero_variance_feature
description: AC-1 edge case: a feature with zero variance over the window. Every SPY day in the 40-day history has the IDENTICAL daily return, so the return feature has zero spread and (because the returns are constant) the rolling-volatility feature is also constant -- both features have zero variance. A z-score standardization divides by the feature's standard deviation; with zero variance that is a division by zero. The fix must guard this degenerate case (e.g. fall back to a zero-centred feature, or skip standardization for the zero-variance dimension) and must NOT emit NaN or Inf or raise. run_monte_carlo must still return a finite probability in [0, 100].
provenance: Schema-derived synthetic history; the constant-return construction deliberately produces the zero-variance degenerate case.
contract: run_monte_carlo returns a finite float in [0, 100]; no NaN, no Inf, no exception. The expected value is asserted as a shape/range invariant, not pinned -- the test does not hardcode a producer-computed probability.
derivation: All SPY daily returns are 0.003 -> rolling vol is constant -> both kNN features have zero variance. A naive (feature - mean)/std produces 0/0 = NaN, which would propagate into the distance and corrupt neighbour selection. The fix must keep the output finite and bounded.
